spec_match.rationale: The page demonstrates a comma-separated `@container` rule with two size/aspect branches, but the chromestatus summary explicitly motivates the feature with feature-query fallbacks (`not-supported(--foo: bar)`) which this demo never exercises.

interactive.rationale: The dashed box is `resize: horizontal`, has two preset-width buttons, and the card's branch readout updates live as the container changes — this is real interactivity.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index promises a `feature query branch when supported and a named-container branch when it isn't` plus a `toggle the support hint` control; the page actually shows only two size/aspect-ratio branches and has no support-toggle UI.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers wide vs narrow but never shows what happens when no branch matches, the API behaviour for >2 comma branches, or the `rule.conditions[]` array that the API section claims will exist.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, the `.ctl-btn` rule overrides background and color together, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-query branch to the comma list
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary uses `--name1 not-supported(--foo: bar), --name2 (width > 600px)` as the canonical example. Extend the rule on this page (or add a sibling demo) to mix a `style()` / `not-supported()` query with a size query so the fallback-against-feature-support angle is visible.

openQuestions.title: Wire up the `toggle the support hint` UI promised in the blurb
openQuestions.detail: The feature-index blurb literally says `toggle the support hint, and watch which branch fires`. There is no such toggle on the page. Either add it (a button that flips a custom property the rule depends on) or rewrite the blurb to match what the page actually does.

openQuestions.title: Surface `CSSContainerRule.conditions[]` live
openQuestions.detail: The `the API` section shows expected output as a comment but never actually calls `document.styleSheets[0].cssRules[...]` and prints the result. A small `read conditions` button that walks the stylesheet and prints the array would close the loop.

openQuestions.title: Show the no-branch-matches state
openQuestions.detail: Currently both width branches always match (one or the other). Add an aspect-ratio combination where neither branch fires so the card returns to its default appearance — that absence is itself worth showing.

summary: The demo is interactive and on-brand visually, but it dodges the spec's headline motivation (feature-support fallbacks via `not-supported()`/`style()`), the blurb references a `support hint toggle` that does not exist on the page, and the `conditions` API is only described in a code block — never actually invoked. Fixing those three would close the gap.
